title: RBAC: Aggregated ClusterRoles
description:
## RBAC: Aggregated ClusterRoles

Your team creates custom ClusterRoles for different personas. You want **any ClusterRole labeled `rbac.example.com/aggregate-to-monitoring: true`** to be automatically merged into a single `monitoring` ClusterRole — without having to update `monitoring` every time a new sub-role is added.

Which Kubernetes feature enables this?

```yaml
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: monitoring
aggregationRule:
  clusterRoleSelectors:
  - matchLabels:
      rbac.example.com/aggregate-to-monitoring: "true"
rules: []
```

options.text: ClusterRoleBinding with a `subjects` list of all sub-roles — the API server merges their rules automatically
Aggregated ClusterRoles — the `aggregationRule.clusterRoleSelectors` field causes the controller to merge rules from matching ClusterRoles into this one
A RoleBinding with `roleRef.kind: ClusterRole` and `roleRef.name: monitoring` applied per namespace
Setting `rules: []` in the ClusterRole causes Kubernetes to inherit all rules from all other ClusterRoles

explanation: **Aggregated ClusterRoles** use the `aggregationRule` field to define a label selector. The `clusterrole-aggregation-controller` watches for ClusterRoles matching those labels and automatically merges their `rules[]` into the aggregating ClusterRole. This pattern is used by the built-in roles (`view`, `edit`, `admin`, `cluster-admin`) — you can extend them by creating a ClusterRole with the right aggregation label. The `rules: []` in the aggregating role is intentionally empty — it gets populated dynamically. Setting `rules: []` alone has no special behavior without `aggregationRule`.

hints.title: Built-in aggregation labels
hints.body: The built-in view/edit/admin roles use aggregation. You can extend them with: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/aggregate-to-view: 'true' etc.
hints.command: kubectl get clusterrole view -o yaml | grep aggregationRule -A 5

hints.title: Inspect the aggregation rule
hints.body: Check if a ClusterRole uses aggregation by looking for the aggregationRule field.
